lines.en_lit: Everyone concedes that if he gained atonement through that was not lost, the one that was lost to die.

However, according to of Rav, This is like designates two sin-offerings as a guarantee, And Rabbi Oshaya said one separated two sin-offerings as a guarantee, he gains atonement through one of them, and the second to graze
Since Rava said Rav holds in accordance with Rabbi Yosei, who said the mitzva with the first from the beginning to be comparable to separated to be lost.

We learned Rabbi Yehuda says: It should die. Granted, according to of Rabbi Yoḥanan, who said second of the first pair should graze, according to Rabbi Yehuda, it should to die, he gains atonement with second of the second pair.

However, according to Rav, who said second of the second pair should graze, according to Rabbi Yehuda it should die, according to Rabbi Yehuda With which will he gain atonement? Do you maintain Rabbi Yehuda was referring to the second of the second pair Rabbi Yehuda was referring to the second of the first pair.
There are those who raised this objection And furthermore, Rabbi Yehuda said: the blood spilled the scapegoat to die. the scapegoat dies, the blood should be spilled,

Granted, according to Rav, the first clause they disagree with regard to a communal sin-offering. And the latter clause they disagree with regard to animals However, according to Rabbi Yoḥanan, what And furthermore, difficult.
And furthermore, Rabbi Yehuda said: the blood spilled the scapegoat should die. Granted, the blood spilled, the scapegoat should die, as the mitzva has not yet been performed,
However, the scapegoat dies, why should the blood be spilled? The mitzva of has been performed.
of the house of Rabbi Yannai said the verse states: shall be stood alive before the Lord, to make atonement Until when must be alive? Until the blood of its counterpart is sprinkled,
We learned there residents of a city sent their shekels and were stolen or were lost if funds were collected, take an oath to the treasurers
And if not take an oath to the residents of the city, and the residents of the city donate other in their stead.
are found or the thieves returned them, both these and those are shekels, but they do not count the following year. Rabbi Yehuda says: They do count toward the following year.
What is the reason for of Rabbi Yehuda? Rava said: Rabbi Yehuda holds the obligations of this year are brought the following year,

Abaye raised an objection to this the bull and goat of Yom Kippur were lost and one designated others in their stead, and similarly the goats idol worship were lost and he designated others in their stead, all should die, the statement of Rabbi Yehuda. Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Shimon say: They should graze until they become unfit. they are sold, and the money their will go to a gift-offering, as a communal sin-offering not die. said to him:
